title: About
text: I’ve spent several years doing research, writing code, and thinking about questions of human choice and decision making. Where I’ve studied and worked is in my résumé — this space is for the thinking.

title: About
text:
Why this, why now?
I read a lot of posts and keep catching myself asking the same thing: why should I trust this? What has the writer actually done, and what analysis stands behind the claim? That question is a large part of why I started writing. I’d rather show the work — the method, the data, the reasoning — than hand over a tidy takeaway and ask you to take my word for it.
I also learn better when I have to explain something to someone other than myself. So this is partly selfish: writing carefully is how I come to understand things properly.

title: About
text:
What is this about?
A space for the curious — people looking for answers to questions we have all run into that don’t quite scratch the itch, who want the finer details of the methods and the results, not just the headline.

title: About
text:
What to expect
A post most Fridays, on a topic in economics, econometrics, statistics, machine learning, artificial intelligence, or computer science. Each one is either a description of a method — how to apply it, when to apply it, why it matters now — or an analysis of a real question worked through with data. I try to make them worth reading: rigorous, but not a slog. Everything is free.

title: The Method Section
text:
Every post here is one of two kinds: a method — how a technique works and when to reach for it — or an applied question worked through with data. Two fields, one standard: show the work.

Economics & econometrics — filter with the Economics chip below.
Computer science & methods — filter with the Computer Science chip below.
Evaluating my work? — my résumé (PDF) has the formal background; the posts themselves are the better sample of how I think.

The category chips below (Economics · Computer Science · Method · Applied) filter the archive along either axis — topic or type of post. They appear as soon as posts are published.
Following along: new posts land on Fridays. Subscribe with any RSS reader via the feed, or plug that feed into a service like Blogtrottr for email — no account with me required.
